description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a symmetrical facade and a prominent, wide front porch supported by columns. The exterior is clad in light gray brickwork, with stone trim at the base and decorative cornices along the roofline. The roof is complex, featuring a combination of hip and gable elements, multiple chimneys, and a decorative railing along the perimeter. Large, rectangular windows are arranged in a balanced pattern on all sides, with the front featuring a central arched window above the main entrance. The entryway is accessed by a short flight of steps and is sheltered by the expansive porch roof. The overall design suggests a blend of early 20th-century American architectural styles, with classical influences in the porch and window detailing.
description: A stately two-story house with light gray brick, a wide front porch, and a complex hip-gable roof with multiple chimneys and decorative railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wide steps, large arched window above the door, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the brickwork, side windows, and the extension of the porch roof to the right.
description_view_2: Rear view with a large covered porch, multiple columns, and a central staircase leading up to the porch.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing symmetrical window placement and the continuation of the stone base and brick facade.
attribute_architectural_style: American Foursquare with Classical Revival elements
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with wide steps, covered porch supported by columns, and an arched window above the main door
